perturbation.original_text: Term . Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the earlier of: (i) two years from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any consummation of the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Term . Unless otherwise provided herein, all obligations under this Agreement shall terminate and cease to have any force or effect on the date of any consummation of the Potential Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the (i) two years from the date hereof, it creates in-text contradiction. Because if a potential transaction is never made, the other sections are still active, thus the pertubation creates an internal contradiction, that makes other sections vague, since it never provides any duration for those sections to expire. For example the Company might not know how long it will keep the Confidential Information according to Section 3, where it relies on the "Term" of Section 2 to expire.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Confidential Information confidential. The Company will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a termination date of either (i) two years from the date hereof, or (ii) the date of any consummation of the Potential Transaction. The changed text removes the two-year termination clause, leaving only the consummation of the potential transaction as the termination date. As the explanation describes, this creates an in-text contradiction with other sections of the contract, such as the confidentiality clause, because the confidentiality clause relies on the 'Term' to define when the confidentiality obligations expire. Now there is no 'Term' if the potential transaction is never consummated, thus contradicting and undermining the confidentiality agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own", there's a loss of clarity. The original clause sets a baseline, ensuring that the Company doesn't treat ABRH's confidential information with any less care than it treats its own. The edited version only requires "reasonable care," which is subjective. This contradiction now occurs with Section 7, "Irreparable Injury; Injunctive Relief", where the original text provides some legal definition to prevent this from happening, thus making Section 7 now open to subjective interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company acknowledges that any unauthorized or wrongful disclosure or use of Confidential Information by the Company, including the Company’s Representatives, or any other breach by the Company, may result in irreparable injury to ABRH that is not adequately compensable in monetary damages. Accordingly, the Company acknowledges that in the event of a breach of this Agreement, ABRH shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need to post any bond and in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity. The Company will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law in the event that ABRH seeks injunctive relief in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text set a higher standard of care (at least the same degree as used for its own information), providing a baseline and potentially limiting the Company's ability to argue 'reasonable care' was met. The changed text, by only requiring 'reasonable care,' introduces ambiguity and lowers the standard, creating a contradiction with Section 7. Section 7 relies on a clear definition of what constitutes a breach that would cause irreparable harm. The modified clause, with its subjective 'reasonable care' standard, now weakens the enforceability of Section 7 by making it harder to prove a breach, thus making Section 7 now open to subjective interpretation. This fulfills the definition of "Omissions – In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: Nature of Relationship . ABRH has no obligation to disclose any Confidential Information that it owns or possesses to the Company or its Representatives under this Agreement. Neither party has any obligation to enter into any transaction with the other. Furthermore, ABRH warrants that it has the right to disclose its own Confidential Information, but does not otherwise make any representation as to their accuracy or completeness. All Confidential Information of ABRH will remain the sole and exclusive property of ABRH. This Agreement grants no rights of ownership, licenses, or any other intellectual property right. Moreover, this Agreement does not create any agency, partnership, joint venture, or any other such relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: Nature of Relationship . Neither party has any obligation to enter into any transaction with the other. All Confidential Information of ABRH will remain the sole and exclusive property of ABRH. This Agreement grants no rights of ownership, licenses, or any other intellectual property right. Moreover, this Agreement does not create any agency, partnership, joint venture, or any other such relationship.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the first and third sentences "ABRH has no obligation to disclose any Confidential Information that it owns or possesses to the Company or its Representatives under this Agreement." and "Furthermore, ABRH warrants that it has the right to disclose its own Confidential Information, but does not otherwise make any representation as to their accuracy or completeness", it implies that ABRH might be obligated to disclose information. This directly contradicts the purpose of the confidentiality agreement stated in the recitals. It also removes ABRH's warranty regarding its right to disclose information, creating doubt. This makes the definition of "Confidential Information" referenced in section 3 ambiguous and creates in-text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS, ABRH and its parent company, Fidelity Newport Holdings, LLC (“FNH”), and the Company are interested in pursuing a potential business transaction (“Potential Transaction”) which would benefit ABRH, FNH, and the Company; WHEREAS, certain ABRH confidential information must be disclosed from time to time to the Company in order to pursue such opportunity; and WHEREAS, ABRH is willing, but only on the terms and conditions set forth below, to disclose its confidential information to the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the statement "ABRH has no obligation to disclose any Confidential Information that it owns or possesses to the Company or its Representatives under this Agreement." This omission, as explained, implies that ABRH might be obligated to disclose information. This directly contradicts the "WHEREAS" clause in the 'Contradicted Text' which states "ABRH is willing, but only on the terms and conditions set forth below, to disclose its confidential information to the Company." By removing the original statement in the 'Original Text', ABRH's willingness to disclose becomes questionable, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Return of Materials . Confidential Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Potential Transaction. Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company shall promptly return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy (except originals, equipment, and devices delivered by ABRH to the Company, which shall be returned to ABRH), all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Confidential Information, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Return of Materials . Upon request of ABRH for any reason, the Company shall promptly return to ABRH, or at Company’s option, destroy all equipment, documents, devices of any kind, or other material of any kind, in any form, containing any Confidential Information, together with all copies, summaries, abstracts, excerpts, extracts, replicas, reproductions, and samples of any of the foregoing, and certify the destruction of any copy or partial copy made.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement "Confidential Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Potential Transaction." and "Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement." This now makes it unclear if the company has the right to reproduce, retain or archive confidentital information. This contradicts section 3, where it implies the company has the right to retain this information. This creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company expressly agrees that it will keep ABRH’s Confidential Information confidential and that neither the Company nor any of its affiliates nor any of its or their directors, officers, employees, operating partners, consultants, or advisors (including without limitation, attorneys and accountants) (collectively, but only to the extent that such persons actually receive Confidential Information, “Representatives”) will use, for its own benefit (other than in connection with the Potential Transaction), or communicate or disclose (orally, in writing, or in any other manner) to any other person or entity any Confidential Information, or that the Company or any of its Representatives have received or otherwise been provided access to Confidential Information by any other party. The Company further agrees that it and its Representatives will not in any way cause or encourage another person to disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to any person or entity or judicial or administrative agency or body for any reason whatsoever unless required to do so pursuant to legal process. The Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information and who are informed of the terms of this Agreement and are directed to keep such Confidential Information confidential. The Company will be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by its Representatives. The Company will not, without ABRH’s written consent, disclose or issue any news release, announcement, denial, or confirmation of this Agreement or any actual or potential business negotiation or relationship between the parties. The Company and its Representatives receiving Confidential Information from ABRH will protect ABRH’s Confidential Information from both unauthorized use and disclosure by exercising at least the same degree of care that is used for similar information of its own, but no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allows the company to reproduce confidential information for evaluation and retain copies for document retention and compliance. The changed text removes these allowances. The contradicted text states the Company will only use ABRH’s Confidential Information for the transaction contemplated above and will only disclose ABRH’s Confidential Information to its Representatives who need to know such information. By removing the statement 'Confidential Information may be reproduced by the Company and Company Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Potential Transaction.' and 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Company may retain such copies of the Confidential Information that reside on the Company’s back-up storage or archiving system, solely for document retention purposes and not for any other use, and for compliance purposes as required by law or to evidence compliance of Company’s obligations under this Agreement.' This now makes it unclear if the company has the right to reproduce, retain or archive confidential information. This removal contradicts the contradicted text, creating ambiguity about the company's rights to use and retain confidential information. This falls under the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as essential language was removed.

perturbation.original_text: Regardless of the above, this provision shall not restrict the right of the Company to solicit or recruit such employees as a result of any substantial asset purchase or sale or merger or through the general use of the media, and the Company shall not be prohibited from hiring such employees who answer any general advertisement or otherwise voluntarily apply for hire without having been personally solicited or recruited by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: This provision shall not restrict the right of the Company to solicit or recruit such employees as a result of any substantial asset purchase or sale or merger or through the general use of the media.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and the Company shall not be prohibited from hiring such employees who answer any general advertisement or otherwise voluntarily apply for hire without having been personally solicited or recruited by the Company." from Section 6, it creates an in-text contradiction. Section 6, Disclosure and Solicitation of Employees, restricts the Company from soliciting ABRH's employees. By removing the second part, it now makes the section contradictory, because it doesn't clarify if the Company can hire such employees without soliciting or recruiting them, creating uncertainty. ABRH can assume that it cannot hire those employees even though that was specified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company and its Representatives will make no contact, written or verbal, with any of ABRH’s management, staff, or employees not directly involved with the transaction contemplated above for purposes of evaluating the 400 West 48th Avenue Denver CO 80216 303.296.2121 Potential Transaction unless with ABRH’s written permission. Further, during the term of eighteen (18) months beginning on the date hereof, the Company shall not knowingly solicit or recruit management or executive level employees of ABRH who became known to the Company through work on the above transaction or purpose contemplated above, without the prior written consent of ABRH. This provision shall not restrict the right of the Company to solicit or recruit such employees as a result of any substantial asset purchase or sale or merger or through the general use of the media.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the company was not prohibited from hiring employees who answered general advertisements or voluntarily applied without being directly solicited. The changed text removes this clarification. The contradicted text restricts the company from soliciting ABRH's employees. With the removal of the clarification in the changed text, it now creates uncertainty whether the company can hire employees who voluntarily apply, which contradicts the intention of the contradicted text, because ABRH could assume the company can not hire such employees even though that was specified, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it is a direct conflict. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction', where essential language is removed.
